revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23985667504|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[fa:قلعه هاکوچی]][[ja:白地城]] {{Infobox Japanese castle | name = Hakuchi Castle | pref = Tokushima Prefecture | img = File:Hakuchi Castle, enkei.jpg | img_capt = Distant view
{{small|Awano-sho (center-right) on the castle site. The Yoshino River (Ikeda Lake) in the foreground.}} | img_width= 280px | ar_called = Hakuchi Ōnishi Castle | struct = Mountain castle | builders = {{ill|Kondō Kyōtei|ja|近藤京帝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kondō Kyōtei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | build_y = {{ill|Jianwu|en|Jianwu|qid=Q11065423|lt=Kenmu|1=Jianwu}} 2 (1335) | revamp = {{ill|Chōsokabe clan|en|Chōsokabe clan|qid=Q981303|lt=Chōsokabe clan|1=Chōsokabe clan}} | rulers = {{ill|Ōnishi clan|ja|大西氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Chōsokabe clan | reject_y = {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|qid=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 13 (1585) | remains = Bailey, earthen walls | cultural asset = Municipal historic site | latd = 34 | latm = 0 | lats = 56.3 | longd = 133 | longm = 46 | longs = 51.6 | map = Japan Tokushima | map_mark = Japanese castle ruins | map_label_position = bottom }} [[File:Hakutizyou01.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Stone marker of Hakuchi Castle ruins}}]] '''Hakuchi Castle''' (白地城) was a {{ill|Japanese castle|en|Japanese castle|qid=Q92026|lt=Japanese castle|1=Japanese castle}} located in Hakuchi, {{ill|Miyoshi District, Tokushima|en|Miyoshi District, Tokushima|qid=Q1156031|lt=Miyoshi District, Awa Province|1=Miyoshi District, Tokushima}}, part of present-day {{ill|Miyoshi, Tokushima|en|Miyoshi, Tokushima|qid=Q990371|lt=Miyoshi, Tokushima|1=Miyoshi, Tokushima}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|en|Tokushima Prefecture|qid=Q160734|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}. The site is designated as a municipal historic site.[http://www.miyoshi.ed.jp/docs/2010112600126/ "List of Municipal Cultural Properties of Miyoshi City"] Miyoshi City Board of Education Official Website == Overview == Hakuchi Castle is renowned as a strategic base for {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|en|Chōsokabe Motochika|qid=Q1070082|lt=Chōsokabe Motochika|1=Chōsokabe Motochika}}, a {{ill|Sengoku daimyo|ja|戦国大名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku daimyo|1=Sengoku daimyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q5384684}} of {{ill|Tosa Province|en|Tosa Province|qid=Q857330|lt=Tosa Province|1=Tosa Province}}, during his campaign to unify {{ill|Shikoku|en|Shikoku|qid=Q13991|lt=Shikoku|1=Shikoku}}. Situated in the mountainous center of Shikoku, the castle commanded access to several key regions: {{ill|Iyo Province|en|Iyo Province|qid=Q907829|lt=Iyo Province|1=Iyo Province}} via the western Sakaime Pass, {{ill|Sanuki Province|en|Sanuki Province|qid=Q929682|lt=Sanuki Province|1=Sanuki Province}} via the northern Inonose Pass, the Yoshino River leading to the central Tokushima Plain in the east, and Tosa Province by heading upstream along the river to the south. It served as a strategic crossroads of the island. == History == Hakuchi Castle was established in {{ill|Jianwu|en|Jianwu|qid=Q11065423|lt=Kenmu|1=Jianwu}} 2 (1335) by Kondō Kyōtei, an estate steward of the Tai Manor (modern {{ill|Ikeda, Tokushima|en|Ikeda, Tokushima|qid=Q626473|lt=Ikeda, Tokushima|1=Ikeda, Tokushima}}). The Kondō family adopted the name Ōnishi and governed the region for eight generations over approximately 250 years.Hakuchi Ōnishi Castle Ruins Guide From the {{ill|Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|en|Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|qid=Q8140349|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)}} to the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|qid=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}, the Ōnishi clan ruled the area around Hakuchi Castle. They initially served the {{ill|Hosokawa clan|en|Hosokawa clan|qid=Q2736120|lt=Hosokawa clan|1=Hosokawa clan}}, the feudal lords of Awa, and later the {{ill|Miyoshi clan|en|Miyoshi clan|qid=Q2975329|lt=Miyoshi clan|1=Miyoshi clan}}, who supplanted the Hosokawa in power. In {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|qid=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 5 (1577), Chōsokabe Motochika, aiming to unify Shikoku, seized Hakuchi Castle due to its strategic importance. The castle's lord at the time, {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi Kakuyō|qq=}}, fled to Masanuki Castle in {{ill|Sanuki Province|en|Sanuki Province|qid=Q929682|lt=Sanuki Province|1=Sanuki Province}}. According to the *Motochika-ki*, Motochika remarked, "Once we take Ōnishi, we shall be at the crossroads of Awa, Sanuki, and Iyo, free to move in any direction." In {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|qid=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 13 (1585), during the {{ill|Shikoku Campaign (Toyotomi)|ja|豊臣の四国攻め|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikoku Campaign (Toyotomi)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} led by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qid=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}, Hakuchi Castle served as a defensive stronghold for the Chōsokabe clan. After their defeat and retreat to Tosa, the castle was abandoned. Following its abandonment, regional governance shifted to the nearby {{ill|Ōnishi Castle (Awa Province)|ja|大西城 (阿波国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi Castle (Awa Province)|1=Ōnishi Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11438844}}. == Ruins == Until the late 20th century, the site featured many relics, including baileys, moats, and {{ill|musha-bashiri|ja|武者走り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/musha-bashiri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (defensive passageways). However, most of these structures were destroyed during the construction of the *Kanpo no Yado* (now Awano-sho). Today, only traces of earthen walls and cut slopes remain in the surrounding area. In the southern section of the Awano-sho property, the Hakuchi Children's Park houses a stone marker for the Hakuchi Castle ruins and the Ōnishi Shrine. File:Hakuchi Castle, setsumeiban.jpg|Awano-sho at the castle site (Hakuchi Castle explanatory board visible on the left) File:Hakutizyouato0ø.jpg|Ōnishi Shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Hakuchi Castle}} * {{ill|List of castles in Japan|en|List of castles in Japan|qid=Q1768024|lt=List of castles in Japan|1=List of castles in Japan}} * {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|en|Chōsokabe Motochika|qid=Q1070082|lt=Chōsokabe Motochika|1=Chōsokabe Motochika}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi Kakuyō|qq=}} * {{ill|Unpenji|ja|雲辺寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unpenji|qq=}} * {{ill|List of castles in Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県の城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of castles in Tokushima Prefecture|1=Castles in Tokushima prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11489854}} {{japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|白地城|version=101627847|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:白地城]] [[ja:地城]] {{Commons category|Hakuchi Castle}} [[fa:قلعه هاکوچی]] {{wikidata link|Q11579474}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Miyoshi, Tokushima]] [[Category:Castles in Awa Province]] [[Category:Castles in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Castles in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Chōsokabe clan]] [[Category:History of Miyoshi, Tokushima]] [[Category:Miyoshi, Tokushima]] [[Category:Nonexistent Architecture of Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
